title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Trace when an incoming request context is canceled and pass it through every request-scoped operation without storing it in long-lived structs.
Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation
steps.instruction:
Order the Cleanup Compensation Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Cleanup Compensation assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation, keep Cleanup Compensation connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 3857 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Cleanup Compensation has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Cleanup Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug team handles case 3857 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.

For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Cleanup Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Deferred cleanup always completes even after the request deadline expires.

options.text: Declare Go server method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Cleanup Compensation case
Predict Go server resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Cleanup Compensation case
Run the smallest deterministic Go server request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Cleanup Compensation case
Inject one changed Go server failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Cleanup Compensation case
